claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements harness boundary audit for full-tool Claude agents with cross-check evidence policy and blocks PRD readiness on boundary violations.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot() hashes all project files outside allowed_write_roots using SHA-256, with git ls-files optimization and symlink detection.
evidence.why: Independent verification confirms boundary baseline creation mechanism exists and matches explorer description.

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots() compares before/after digests to detect added/modified/deleted files, returning sorted changes with path and change type.
evidence.why: Confirms violation detection logic matches explorer claim.

evidence.summary: execution_policy sets project_mutation='forbidden' and allowed_write_roots=[output_dir] in dispatch plan.
evidence.why: Verifies policy-level enforcement of non-mutation constraint.

evidence.summary: runDemandEvidenceDispatchRuntime() builds boundary snapshots before/after agent execution, detects changes via diffBoundarySnapshots, sets project_mutation status, and creates BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers for each violation.
evidence.why: Runtime enforcement loop confirms boundary audit is active during agent execution.

evidence.summary: boundaryBlockers map each detected change to BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blocker with path and change type, then merged into readiness.blockers, forcing prd_ready=false.
evidence.why: Direct integration between boundary violations and PRD readiness gate confirmed.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS defines explorer, cross_checker, and verifier with writes_code=false and result_schema.
evidence.why: Confirms evidence agent protocol specification matches implementation.

evidence.summary: evidence_policy routing: technicalRisk or highCost triggers 'cross_check'; existing projects get 'single_agent'; greenfield gets 'none'.
evidence.why: Verifies cross-check policy triggers on high-risk signals as explorer claimed.

evidence.summary: inspectDemandPrdReadiness() checks for each required evidence role that at least one result is complete, adding EVIDENCE_AGENT_RESULT_REQUIRED blockers otherwise.
evidence.why: PRD readiness gate enforcement matches explorer description.

evidence.summary: Test 'boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass' creates unexpected-project-write.txt during agent run, asserts status=blocked, project_mutation=violated, and BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blocker present.
evidence.why: Test evidence independently verifies boundary enforcement works as claimed.

evidence.summary: Tests 'git-backed boundary detects untracked project mutations' and 'gitignored project mutations' verify git ls-files integration and gitignore-aware filesystem walk.
evidence.why: Confirms boundary works with both git and non-git repos, matching explorer claim.

evidence.summary: gitFiles() runs 'git ls-files -co --exclude-standard -z' for project file list; walkFiles() provides fallback for non-git repos.
evidence.why: Provides deeper verification of boundary implementation details.

evidence.summary: boundaryEntryDigest() handles symlinks via readlinkSync(), files via SHA-256 hash, and other nodes via mode/size/mtimeMs tuple.
evidence.why: Independent verification of symlink detection and digest strategy.

evidence.summary: buildProviderInvocation() constructs Claude/codex/custom provider commands with -p flag, permission-mode, settings, tools, and budget controls for full-tool agent spawning.
evidence.why: Verifies provider abstraction used by evidence dispatch for full-tool agents.

risks: Boundary snapshot uses filesystem timestamp (mtimeMs) for non-file nodes; rapid filesystem changes could potentially miss mutations within the same millisecond.
Git ls-files -co flag includes untracked files but may miss files in submodules if not configured properly.
Symlink detection reads link target but does not prevent symlink-based path traversal attacks if agent creates malicious symlinks pointing outside project.
allowed_write_roots path resolution uses isWithin() with relative() check; complex symlinks or Windows UNC paths could create false negatives.

result.notes: Independent verification confirms all explorer claims. Evidence dispatch implements boundary audit via buildBoundarySnapshot/diffBoundarySnapshots with SHA-256 hashing, git/non-git fallback, and symlink detection. Execution policy sets project_mutation='forbidden'. Runtime enforcement builds snapshots before/after agents and creates BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers that merge into PRD readiness, blocking completion. Cross-check policy triggers on technical/high-cost risks. Tests verify git/non-git boundary detection. Provider adapter confirms full-tool agent spawning capability.
